full_text:
Färkkilä-GSK collaboration on STING agonist GSK3859856

Project update
12.09.2023

High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC)

Most common (~70%) and lethal subtype of ovarian cancers
 ~ 80% of the patients exhibit omental metastasis


Frequent genetic alterations
homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) (~50%)
mutation in TPS3, BRCA1/2 genes (~20%)


Potential targeted therapies
PARPi effective against HRD tumors
immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) response rate is 8-10%
better response to combination of PARPi plus anti-PD-1 in HRD HGSC

Nieman K.M et al., 2011
Cojocaru et al., 2017
Färkkilä et al., 2020

Research objective:

To test STING agonist (GSK3859856) alone and in combinations with anti- PD-1 antibody, PARP inhibitor and other agents on patient-derived immunocompetent cultures (iPDCs) established from HGSC patient’s undergoing surgery at Helsinki University Hospital.

Readouts:
 a) Cytotoxic response from tumor and immune cells using image-based analysis 
 b) Abundance and functional states of immune cells using multiparameter flow cytometry

Novel patient-derived matrix improves the growth of HGSC patient-derived cultures while preserving the histology and genomic features of the source tumors

Source tumor-derived immune cells are preserved in the OmGel cultures

iPDCs

iPDCs show HRD genotype-specific response to PARPi and anti-PD1 treatments

Workflow for testing novel combination therapies on HGSC patient-derived immunocompetent cultures

Sample set summary

Evaluation of cytotoxic and functional response from tumor and immune cells

Log2FC in the proportion of live CK7+ live cells per treatment normalized to control
Effective killing dose for cancer cells (ED75): proportion of images per treatment in which at least 75% of cancer cells are dead, normalized to control
3. Cancer cell-specific death: (CK7+ dead) - (CD45+ dead)) / all cells per treatment normalized to control

STINGa in combination with ATRi induce cancer cell death

Ratio of CK7+ live / all cells normalized to DMSO

ATRi as a single agent or in combination with STINGa induce cancer cell-specific death regardless of HR status

Decrease in the number of CD8+ T cells with increasing concentration
Subtle increase in IFNg in dendritic cells and monocytes
Increased GrzB in NKT cells

Effect of STINGa dosage on immune cell abundance and functional states

Upon STINGa treatment :
Decrease in dendritic cells in the lower concentrations 
Fewer CD8+ T cells in the tumor and cultures 
Subtle increase in GrzB in T cells and IFNg in monocyets

Effect of STINGa on immune cell abundance and functional states

HRP

Non-responder

Summary

STINGa as a single agent does not induce significant death in cancer cells

Combination of STINGa with ATRi is effective in inducing cancer cell-specific death with no significant cytotoxic effect on immune cells

Higher does of STINGa decrease the proportion of T cells in some samples

Responding samples show higher proportion of CD8+ T cells compared to non-responders

Discussion points

Publication plans: manuscript review and acknowledgements
Preparation of the final report: specific guidelines and formats
How to deal with the left over compound
